title: avena bromoides subsp. requienii
intro: Avena bromoides subsp. requienii, also known as the false oat-grass or oat-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits associated with Avena bromoides subsp. requienii include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. Avena bromoides subsp. requienii can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds in a solvent.
